title: Systems and methods for protecting the cerebral vasculature
abstract: Vascular filters and deflectors and methods for filtering bodily fluids. A blood filtering assembly can capture embolic material dislodged or generated during an endovascular procedure to inhibit or prevent the material from entering the cerebral vasculature. A blood deflecting assembly can deflect embolic material dislodged or generated during an endovascular procedure to inhibit or prevent the material from entering the cerebral vasculature.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. An embolic protection system for isolating the cerebral vasculature, the system comprising: a first protection device having a proximal portion configured to remain outside the body and a distal portion, the distal portion comprising: an outer sheath; and a first self-expanding filter assembly radially within the outer sheath; and a second protection device having a proximal portion configured to remain outside the body and a distal portion, the distal portion comprising: a proximal sheath; a proximal self-expanding filter assembly radially within the proximal sheath; a distal sheath; and a distal self-expanding filter assembly radially within the distal sheath. 2. The embolic protection system of claim 1 , wherein the first self-expanding filter assembly includes a proximally facing opening. 3. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-2, wherein the proximal self- expanding filter assembly includes a distally facing opening. 4. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-3, wherein the distal self- expanding filter assembly includes a proximally facing opening. 5. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-4, wherein the first protection device further comprises a filter wire coupled to a proximal end of the first self-expanding filter and extending distally therefrom. 6. The embolic protection system of claim 5, wherein the second protection device further comprises a lumen configured to receive the filter wire of the first protection device. 7. The embolic protection system of claim 6, wherein the lumen extends over less than an entire length of the second protection device. 8. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 6-7, wherein the lumen is in communication with a rapid exchange port proximally spaced from a distal end of the distal sheath. 9. The embolic protection system of claim 6, wherein the lumen extends an entirety of a length of the second protection device. 10. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-9, wherein the first protection device further comprises an inner member radially inward of the outer sheath. 11. The embolic protection system of claim 10, wherein the inner member comprises a guidewire lumen. 12. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-11, wherein at least one of the first or second protection devices are connected to an arterial pressure monitoring device. 13. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the distal sheath is articulatable. 14. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-13, wherein each of the first self-expanding filter, the proximal self-expanding filter, and the distal self-expanding filter are configured to be individually deployed. 15. The embolic protection system of any one of claims 1-14, further comprises a first handle assembly coupled to the proximal portion of the first embolic protection device and a second handle assembly coupled to the proximal portion of the second embolic protection device.
